evidence.kpi_link: Visitor numbers at countryside sites, income per site, camping occupancy, sportive participation

implementation_steps.step: Identify all LCC countryside land along TdF route suitable for camping/events
Apply for planning permission for temporary camping at 5-8 sites
Tender catering and hospitality concessions for climb viewing points
Develop and market Lancashire Cycling Sportive for July 2027 and annually thereafter
Coordinate with Marketing Lancashire on cycling tourism legacy programme

evidence.political_framing: The Tour de France puts Lancashire's countryside on the global stage. We turn that into lasting income: camping, events, an annual sportive. Not a one-off; a permanent new revenue stream.

operational_context.landfill_rate_note: 33.8% to landfill, approximately 6x national average. EfW strategy vacuum is primary cause.
operational_context.waste_strategy_status: Expired 2020. No successor published. EfW procurement paused.

portfolios.title: Data, Technology, Customer & Efficiency
directors: Peter Lloyd, Director of Digital

budget_latest.note: Shared with Resources portfolio, ICT and digital within Central Services budget

statutory_duties.summary: Data protection and information governance
statutory_duties.risk: ICO enforcement: fines up to £17.5M or 4% of turnover. Oracle data breaches in 2022 migration.

statutory_duties.summary: Respond to FOI requests within 20 working days
statutory_duties.risk: Must comply but can charge for complex requests

statutory_duties.summary: Digital procurement systems and compliance
statutory_duties.risk: Live from 24 February 2025. Dynamic Markets replace DPS. System changes needed.

key_services: ICT infrastructure and systems (Oracle Fusion ERP, Liquid Logic social care, Nutanix)
AI and automation (AI used in EHCP compliance checking, highway inspections piloted)
known_pressures: Oracle Fusion ERP: catastrophic 2022 migration, now stabilised. Data breaches, payment delays, salary overpayments. 100% empty transaction descriptions (transparency score 0.0).
Softcat £64M IT infrastructure contract (4 years from Dec 2025), £10.4M in 20 months of spending data
Netcall £4.3M agentic AI / CRM contract, new investment
Cyber security threats increasing
Legacy systems: Liquid Logic (social care), Oracle Fusion (finance), integration challenges
Customer expectations for digital self-service
AI and automation opportunities not yet fully captured
Procurement Act 2023 compliance: system and process changes needed

demand_pressures.detail: 100% of 713,596 transactions have empty descriptions (transparency score 0.0). Oracle ERP export limitation. Single biggest barrier to forensic analysis and public accountability.
Oracle Fusion (finance, still bearing scars of 2022), Liquid Logic (social care), Nutanix (infrastructure). Integration gaps. CQC cited 'unreliable and hard to use' electronic records.
AI used in EHCP compliance checking and highway inspections but not yet systematically applied to procurement, fraud detection, customer routing, report generation.

key_contracts.name: Softcat IT infrastructure
key_contracts.note: Core IT infrastructure and managed services. £10.4M in 20 months of data.

key_contracts.name: Netcall AI/CRM
key_contracts.note: Agentic AI and CRM platform. Customer service transformation.

key_contracts.name: Oracle Fusion ERP
key_contracts.value: Unknown, Oracle credits of £162K and £150K noted
key_contracts.note: Core finance system. KPMG review classified exempt.

savings_levers.description: Every transaction shifted from phone/face-to-face to digital saves £5-10 per interaction. Target 80% digital for routine services. Netcall AI/CRM investment should enable this.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Must maintain accessibility for digitally excluded, Equality Act 2010

data_points: SOCITM benchmark: digital transaction costs 3-20x less than phone/face-to-face
AI already proven in EHCP compliance and highway inspections
evidence.benchmark: SOCITM: digital transaction £0.08, phone £2.83, face-to-face £8.62. Leading councils achieve 80%+ digital for routine transactions
evidence.calculation: Estimated 500,000 routine transactions/yr. If 40% shift from phone (£2.83) to digital (£0.08) = 200,000 × £2.75 saving = £550K. Including staff redeployment from contact centre: £2-5M
evidence.kpi_link: CQC cited need for better digital systems. Channel shift frees staff to focus on complex cases rather than routine enquiries

implementation_steps.step: Deploy Netcall AI/CRM for top 10 routine transaction types
Design self-service portal for ASC, SEND, highways reporting
Chatbot for FAQs and simple enquiries (AI-powered)
Redeploy contact centre staff to complex case handling

evidence.political_framing: We've invested £4.3 million in AI and CRM technology. Now we need to use it. Digital services are faster for residents and save the council £5-10 per transaction.

savings_levers.description: Multiple overlapping systems across directorates. Consolidate onto fewer platforms, eliminate duplicate licences. Oracle Fusion stabilised, now build on it.

data_points: Softcat £64M IT infrastructure (4-year contract from Dec 2025)
Oracle Fusion ERP stabilised post-2022 catastrophe
evidence.benchmark: NAO estimates UK public sector spends 30% more than necessary on IT due to duplicate systems. Leading councils consolidate to 3-5 core platforms
evidence.calculation: Estimated 15-20 major systems across LCC. Decommissioning 5 duplicate systems at avg £200K/yr licence + £100K support = £1.5M. Migration costs offset by Softcat contract scope
evidence.kpi_link: CQC cited 'unreliable electronic records'. System consolidation directly addresses data quality. Oracle transparency fix (0.0 score) requires cleaner system architecture

implementation_steps.step: Complete IT systems audit: map all applications, licences, and costs by directorate
Identify top 5 duplicate/overlapping systems for decommission
Migrate data to core platforms (Oracle Fusion, Liquid Logic, Netcall)
Terminate duplicate licences and support contracts

evidence.political_framing: The council runs dozens of overlapping IT systems, each with its own licence fee. We're consolidating to save money and fix the data chaos left by the Oracle debacle.

savings_levers.description: Automate routine processes: FOI triage, invoice matching, customer routing, report generation. Already proven in EHCP compliance and highway inspections. Each automated process saves 0.5-2 FTE.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Public sector equality duty: AI decisions must be fair and auditable

data_points: AI already proven in EHCP compliance checking
13,258 employees, many doing routine data processing
Oracle Fusion stabilised, ready for automation
evidence.benchmark: UK Government Automation Strategy: 30% of government tasks automatable. AWS estimates £1.7B saving for UK public sector through AI
evidence.calculation: Each automated process saves 0.5-2 FTE (avg £35K/yr). Target 30 processes = 30 FTE equivalent = £1.05M. Including productivity gains for remaining staff: £1-3M
evidence.kpi_link: CQC cited unreliable electronic records. AI data quality checking directly addresses this. DfE SEND improvement plan includes AI EHCP compliance, already proven

implementation_steps.step: Identify top 30 automatable processes across all directorates
Deploy AI for FOI triage and response drafting
Automated invoice matching and duplicate detection in Oracle
AI report generation for scrutiny committees
Scale highway AI inspections from pilot to full network

evidence.political_framing: AI is already checking EHCP compliance and inspecting our highways. We'll extend it to invoice processing, customer service, and report writing. It's not replacing people, it's freeing them up for the work that matters.

savings_levers.description: Fix Oracle ERP export to include transaction descriptions. This is the single most important fix for accountability. Currently 0.0 transparency score, 100% empty.

data_points: Oracle Fusion ERP catastrophic 2022 migration is root cause
KPMG review classified exempt from publication
Other councils using Oracle Fusion do export descriptions
evidence.benchmark: 12 district councils in Lancashire achieve 50-95% description completeness. LCC at 0% is an outlier. Best practice councils achieve 95%+ description coverage
evidence.calculation: Zero direct saving but enables: duplicate detection (£93.6M flagged), procurement analysis (HHI, competition), departmental accountability, public transparency compliance
evidence.kpi_link: Local Government Transparency Code 2015 requires itemised spending data. Empty descriptions arguably breach the Code's spirit. ICO has issued guidance on spending data quality

implementation_steps.step: Audit Oracle Fusion export configuration: identify why descriptions are stripped
Configure export to include purchase order description and cost centre name
Back-populate descriptions from purchase orders for last 2 years
Publish corrected spending data with full descriptions

evidence.political_framing: Every other council in Lancashire publishes transaction descriptions. LCC publishes nothing: 713,000 transactions with no explanation. We're fixing this because taxpayers deserve to know where their money goes.

savings_levers.description: LCC operates on-premise server rooms costing £3-5M annually (hardware, power, cooling, staff). Migrating to cloud (Azure/AWS) reduces capital refresh cycles, cuts energy costs, and enables pay-per-use scaling. 40% cost reduction is typical for similar migrations.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Data protection (UK GDPR, sovereign data requirements), PSN compliance, cyber security standards

data_points: On-premise server rooms across multiple sites
Power and cooling costs rising with energy prices
evidence.benchmark: Hackney LBC saved £1.5M from cloud migration. UK Government Cloud First policy since 2013. 60% of councils now cloud-first.
evidence.calculation: £3-5M annual on-premise cost. Cloud equivalent: 60% of current cost. Saving: £1.2-2M. Net of migration costs (year 1): £0.5-2M.
evidence.kpi_link: IT service availability, cyber security audit, NCSC Cyber Essentials Plus

implementation_steps.step: Audit all on-premise systems and data classification
Design cloud migration roadmap (cloud-first for new, lift-and-shift for legacy)
Migrate first tranche (email, collaboration, file storage)
Migrate remaining systems and decommission server rooms

evidence.political_framing: The government's own policy is cloud-first. LCC is still running expensive server rooms. We are moving to the cloud, cutting costs, and improving resilience.

savings_levers.lever: Robotic Process Automation for back office
savings_levers.description: RPA automates repetitive manual tasks: invoice processing, HR onboarding paperwork, FOI response collation, benefit calculations, data entry. Automating 20 high-volume processes frees up staff time equivalent to 15-30 FTE, redeployable or removed through natural wastage.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: No specific legal barrier, subject to data protection and audit trail requirements

evidence.benchmark: Sunderland CC automated 35 processes saving £1.2M. Stoke-on-Trent £0.8M from 15 bots. RPA typically delivers 3:1 ROI within 12 months.
evidence.calculation: 20 processes automated. Average saving per process: 0.5-1.5 FTE. Total: 10-30 FTE x £30K = £300K-900K salary. Plus error reduction and speed: £0.5-1.5M.
evidence.kpi_link: Processing times, error rates, staff redeployment tracking

implementation_steps.step: Identify top 20 processes by volume, repetitiveness, and error rate
Procure RPA platform (UiPath, Blue Prism, or Power Automate)
Build and test first 5 automations (invoices, HR, FOI)
Roll out remaining 15 automations with staff redeployment plan

evidence.political_framing: Software robots can process invoices in seconds. Humans should not be doing data entry when machines do it better and cheaper. We are freeing up staff for work that actually needs a human.

savings_levers.description: LCC holds hundreds of software licences. Audit typically reveals 15-25% shelfware (licences paid for but unused), duplicate tools serving the same function, and enterprise agreements priced above market. A licence audit and renegotiation saves £0.3-1M.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Software contract terms, vendor lock-in considerations

data_points: Enterprise agreements renewed on rolling basis
evidence.benchmark: SOCITM benchmark: average council wastes 20% of software spend on unused licences. Birmingham CC saved £0.8M from licence audit.
evidence.calculation: Estimated £5-8M annual software spend. 15-20% shelfware = £0.75-1.6M. Conservative after renegotiation: £0.3-1M.
evidence.kpi_link: IT spend per user, software utilisation metrics, SOCITM benchmarking

implementation_steps.step: Deploy licence management tool to audit all installed software
Identify unused licences, duplicate tools, and over-provisioned seats
Cancel unused licences and consolidate duplicate tools
Renegotiate enterprise agreements at renewal dates

evidence.political_framing: We are paying for software nobody uses. A proper audit will find the waste and cut it.

savings_levers.description: LCC still prints and posts thousands of letters, reports, and documents that could be sent electronically. Making digital the default for all non-statutory communications reduces print, paper, and postage costs.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Statutory notices must still be served in prescribed form (some require paper). Equalities duty for digitally excluded residents

data_points: Estimated £1-2M annual print and post budget
Digital channel exists for most communication types
evidence.benchmark: Plymouth CC cut print budget by 40% through digital-by-default policy. Most councils now digital-first.
evidence.calculation: £1-2M print/post budget. 30-40% reduction through digital default = £0.3-0.8M. Net of digital exclusion mitigation: £0.2-0.5M.
evidence.kpi_link: Print volume tracking, postage spend, digital channel adoption rate

implementation_steps.step: Audit all printed communications by volume, cost, and statutory requirement
Set digital-by-default policy for all non-statutory communications
Migrate remaining paper workflows to digital (email, portal, app)
Maintain paper option for statutory notices and digitally excluded

evidence.political_framing: Printing and posting letters costs a fortune. Email is free. Unless the law says we must send paper, we will send digital.

savings_levers.lever: Data monetisation and analytics as a service
savings_levers.description: LCC holds vast datasets: traffic flows, planning decisions, social care demand, education outcomes, population demographics, highway conditions, property data, and waste volumes. Anonymised, aggregated data products can be sold to property developers, insurance companies, retail analysts, academic researchers, and neighbouring authorities. Start with anonymised traffic count data (already public via DfT) packaged with LCC-specific analysis. Expand to planning analytics dashboards for developers, footfall data for retailers, and benchmarking services for other councils.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: UK GDPR and Data Protection Act 2018 (anonymisation required, DPIA for any personal data, ICO guidance on anonymisation code of practice), LGA 2003 s95 trading powers (must be through company for commercial trading), Freedom of Information Act 2000 (some data already public), Re-use of Public Sector Information Regulations 2015 (RPSI - right to charge marginal cost, or full cost if 'trading fund')

data_points: LCC holds 20+ years of highway, planning, and social data
Traffic count data alone covers 1,011 DfT count points in Lancashire
Property developers routinely pay for local authority data packs
Academic sector values longitudinal council datasets
evidence.benchmark: Ordnance Survey generates GBP 165M/yr from public sector data. TfL generates GBP 3M from data licensing. Smaller councils (Stockport, Warrington) generate GBP 50-100K from planning data packs.
evidence.calculation: Planning data packs for developers (200 at GBP 500-1K): GBP 100-200K. Traffic analytics subscriptions (50 at GBP 2-5K): GBP 100-250K. Benchmarking service to other councils (10 at GBP 10-20K): GBP 100-200K. Academic data licensing: GBP 30-50K. Total: GBP 0.2-0.8M.
evidence.kpi_link: Data product revenue, customer count, data quality scores, ICO compliance

implementation_steps.step: Audit all LCC datasets for commercial potential and anonymisation requirements
Complete DPIA for each data product and obtain DPO sign-off
Launch planning data packs as first product (low risk, high demand)
Develop traffic analytics dashboard for subscription sale
Explore data partnership with UCLan or Lancaster University

evidence.political_framing: We sit on decades of valuable data that commercial companies would pay for. Properly anonymised, this is a zero-risk income stream. We are not selling personal data; we are selling insights.

cross_portfolio_dependencies.dependency: Shared budget. Oracle fix enables all portfolios' financial accountability.
Digital care technology and self-service portals. Liquid Logic improvements directly impact ASC waiting lists.
EHCP online portal. AI compliance checking. SEND transport routing optimisation.
AI highway inspections. Route optimisation for winter maintenance and SEND transport.
